title: Tapping During the Panic
subtitle: A five-minute tapping practice to use when anxiety spikes suddenly
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice for the moment when anxiety spikes without warning. Designed to be used during or immediately after the physical rush of panic, when the body needs a concrete action to hold on to.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s.

practice.whenToUse: Use when anxiety spikes suddenly. Also useful in the minutes after a panic episode when the body is coming back down.
practice.whenNotToUse: Not a substitute for medical assessment if panic attacks are frequent or severe. If you are experiencing chest pain, difficulty breathing, or any symptom that may indicate a medical emergency, call emergency services.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice for the moment when anxiety spikes out of nowhere. The script gives the nervous system something concrete to do while the physical wave moves through. Use it during the spike or in the minutes immediately after.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop (repeat x3)
Even though my body is doing something I didn't choose and it feels frightening, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though this panic arrived without warning and I don't know why, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I feel out of control of what is happening in my body right now, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
**Eyebrow:** This came out of nowhere.
**Side of eye:** My heart is going fast.
**Under eye:** I am scared of what my body is doing.
**Under nose:** My body is in alarm right now.
**Chin:** I don't know how to make this stop.
**Collarbone:** Everything feels too much.
**Under arm:** I want this to be over.
**Top of head:** I am right in the middle of this.
Reframe to positive
**Eyebrow:** This is a panic response. My body is not broken.
**Side of eye:** This feeling is going to pass.
**Under eye:** I am breathing. My feet are on the floor.
**Under nose:** My body is doing something it was built to do in perceived danger.
**Chin:** The peak of this is already here.
**Collarbone:** I can tap through this.
**Under arm:** I have come through this before.
**Top of head:** This is temporary. I am coming down.
Tap the karate chop three times, then the tapping round three times, then the reframe three times. Repeat as many times as needed. The physical action of tapping is its own point of return.
Where this practice comes from
Original to homemade.education. The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s from Roger Callahan's Thought Field Therapy.
